action.reasoning: Observation: best 0.594 (iter16: replay=500/task balanced via meta+torch.where/randperm AFTER task train, KD beta=0.5 T=2.0 with KLDiv on log_softmax(new/T) vs softmax(old/T)*T**2, consol=5@1e-4, Adam(5e-4/1e-5), cosine per-task, dense logs every20steps+val/best per epoch, deepcopy old_model, final best ckpt). Iters17-24 all failed (0.58 down to 0.08) due to persistent train.py truncation (incomplete after combined_dataset creation; missing full loop/opt/KD/scheduler/mem_update_after_task/consol/best-tracking; late tasks loss~11-12 train_acc~0.04 random, val~0.07). Analysis: workspace truncation of long files is root cause (current train.py ends at 'train_'); successful runs had complete code with post-task memory update on current task only (grows to 5000), proper KD only post-task0, per-epoch val+best on val.pt, CosineAnnealing reset per-task. Replay+KD+consol+best-val proven at ~0.59 with balanced task accs (early~0.48 late~0.7-0.8). Decision: fully rewrite COMPLETE concise non-truncated train.py exactly matching iter16 params (mem=500/task, epochs/task=6, consol=5@1e-4, Adam5e-4, KD beta0.5/T2.0 correct KL, cosine per-task, dense logging+val/best per epoch, mem update AFTER each task, final best ckpt with model_state_dict). Keep model.py/predict.py. This recovers ~0.59+; run training then submit_eval on checkpoint.pt.
